title: leucheria integrifolia
intro: Leucheria integrifolia, also known as mountain tansy or mountain everlasting, is a perennial plant that belongs to the asteraceae family. It is commonly found in the mountainous regions of New Zealand and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is rich in volatile oils and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Leucheria integrifolia include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
